name: Fatfree Chinese Dumplings
recipeIngredient: 2 cups shredded vegetables (a mixture of mostly cabbage or bok choy, carrots, mushrooms, onion; pre-packaged coleslaw mix works well)
A dozen wonton/dumpling skins (made from flour and water)
Fresh garlic, finely chopped or powdered
Fresh ginger, finely chopped or powdered
Small bowl with water for dipping
Optional: 1-2 tablespoons Hunan chili paste, 1 tablespoon plum sauce, or a teaspoon of black bean sauce and five spice powder
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the dipping sauce first by mixing equal parts soy sauce and vinegar (about 1/8 cup per person). Also, mix equal parts garlic and ginger (about a clove's worth per person).
2. Stir-fry the vegetable mixture in a small amount of water and soy sauce. If desired, add your preferred optional flavoring (such as chili paste). Stir until translucent.
3. To assemble the dumplings, scoop about 1.5 teaspoons of the filling onto a dumpling skin. Experiment to find the right amount. Fold the dumpling into a neat shape and dip the end into the small bowl of water to seal it.
4. Cook the dumplings by boiling for 3-5 minutes or steaming for 5-10 minutes, until they crinkle up and turn translucent.
5. Dip the dumplings in the sauce before eating. Enjoy!
